line_technical_records.color_type: Permanent cream haircolor ('Permanent Color'; Certified Vegan; Pre-Bonded, Pre-Blended, Pre-Paired)
line_technical_records.default_mixing_ratio: 1:1 (Blended, Reflect, Extra Coverage, DreamAge, HD, SoColor 10 min collections); 1:2 (Ultra Blonde); 3:1 SoColor:additive (Extra Blonding Cream and SoRed booster uses); SoRed highlighter 1:1
developer_strengths_if_stated: 10 Volume: Up to 1 Level of lightening action
20 Volume: Up to 2 Levels of lightening action
30 Volume: Up to 3 Levels of lightening action
40 Volume: Up to 4 Levels of lightening action
'In general, when lifting beyond one level, for every 10 Volume of Matrix Cream Developer used, you will achieve up to one level of lift.'
Ultra Blonde: 30 Volume (9%) for maximum neutralization, 40 Volume (12%) for maximum lift
After a texture service (post 14-day wait): may not exceed 20 volume
processing_time_ranges: Blended Collection: 30-45 minutes room temperature (*full 45 minutes when lifting more than 1 level and when depositing on gray); FAQ states 'Blended: 30min'
Reflect Collection: 30-45 minutes room temperature; FAQ states 'Reflect: 30min'
Extra Coverage: up to 45 minutes room temperature (*full 45 minutes for resistant gray hair); FAQ states 'Extra Coverage: 30-45min'
DreamAge: up to 45 minutes room temperature; FAQ states 'Dream Age: 30-45min'
Ultra Blonde: 50 minutes room temperature (*full 50 minutes for maximum lift + neutralization)
HD Reds: 30-45 minutes room temperature; FAQ states 'HD Reds: 30min'
SoColor 10 min: 10 minutes (5-minute buffer window between minutes 10-15)
SoRed: highlighter use up to 15 minutes with heat + 5 minutes cool; booster use 30 minutes room temperature
Extra Blonding Cream formulas: 30-45 minutes room temperature
Base Adjust technique: 5-10 minutes including application
gray_coverage_rules: Blended: up to 50% gray coverage; formulated for natural and fashion tones on <50% gray
Extra Coverage: up to 100% gray coverage; formulated for >50% gray
DreamAge: low ammonia, up to 100% gray coverage ('100% Multi-Dimensional Gray Coverage')
Reflect: formulated for up to 50% gray coverage / '<50% Gray' (At a Glance)
Power Cools: up to 50% gray coverage
HD Series: does not cover gray
SoColor 10 min: 100% gray coverage in 10 minutes
lift_rules: Extra Coverage: up to 2 levels of lift
Blended: up to 4 levels of lift
Reflect: up to 4 levels of lift
UL-Blondes: up to 5 levels of lift
HD Reds: up to 4 levels of lift
Power Cools: up to 4 levels of lift
Dream Age: up to 2 levels of lift

tone_family_legend.meaning: Indicates that it covers over 50% gray hair.
The letters indicated the primary and secondary tones
Extra Blonding Cream - 'Additive for SoColor permanent hair color that helps disperse natural underlying pigment so blondes are cleaner, reds are brighter and browns are richer.'

special_usage_notes: Apply to clean dry hair.
Pre-Blended with brown-to-tan background: deeper levels have deeper brown background, medium levels light brown, lighter levels tan/beige.
Virgin application: mids 10 min, base 10 min, ends remaining time (30 min total deposit only; 45 min lift + deposit).
Blending through mids + ends only recommended with Blended + Reflect Series; do not blend Extra Coverage, DreamAge or UL through ends.

Do not refresh with SoColor if: base formula mixed with 20 Volume or higher (10 Volume ideal for deposit-only refreshing); high porosity; base formula is Extra Coverage, DreamAge or UL Series; maximum neutralization needed (use a demi-permanent).

Ultra Blonde shades not intermixable with other SoColor shades; HD Series not mixable with any other SoColor shade (turns purple with naturals); Extra Coverage intermixable with Blended & Reflect (may reduce gray coverage); DreamAge mixing with non-DreamAge not recommended; SoColor 10 min only with other 10 min shades.
'The + in the Red series means more vibrancy & tone; the + in the Ultra-Blondes series means more lift.'
Wait at least 14 days after heat straightening / sodium hydroxide / guanidine / calcium relaxer before using SoColor; then do not exceed 20 volume.
Overly porous hair: lightly mist Instacure on mids + ends before pulling through.
Base Adjust formulas by natural level: levels 1-4 use 6AA + 20 Volume; levels 5-6 use 8AV or 8AA + 20 Volume; levels 7-8 use 10AV + 20 Volume.
Shine and condition last up to 30 shampoos.
Several Blended palettes (Warm, Blended Brown, Blended Bronze, Gold shades) marked 'Launching June 2023'.
line_technical_records.source_document: 2023 Matrix Manual Haircolor (official PDF, 82 pages, cover: '2023 Manual Haircolor')
assumptions: SoColor 10 min Pre-Bonded shades included in this line per the manual FAQ listing 'SoColor 10 min' as one of SoColor's 8 technologies, although it is also marketed as a separately named product.
data_gaps: The manual prints the reflect/tone legend keyed to digits (.0-.9), not to a letter-by-letter table; letter meanings beyond those explicitly decoded (UL, A+, SR, R, HD, EBC, SP, N/J primary-secondary) are not printed and were not inferred.

Fundamentals section (p.155) prints a variant digit legend: '.3 Gold/Warm' and '.5 Brown' versus '.3 Gold' and '.5 Mahogany' in the SoColor/SoColor Sync decoding sections (pp.57-58, 107-108). Decoding-section wording used in tone_family_legend.

Manual 'At a Glance' shade counts (Blended 58, Reflect 18, Extra Coverage 22, DreamAge 10, Ultra Blonde 8, SoColor 10 min 10) do not all match palette-chart enumeration (Blended 62 codes incl. Power Cools, Reflect 10 codes + 7 specialty); the discrepancy could not be resolved from the manual.
No per-shade names, swatch confirmations, or discontinued-status statements in the manual.

Official matrix.com product page also lists 'High Impact Brunettes' (1:1.5, 35 minutes) and 'Reflect Collection Metallics' (1:1 with 20 or 30 volume, 35 minutes) sub-collections that do not appear in the 2023 manual; their shades could not be enumerated (page shade list is JavaScript-rendered).

line_technical_records.color_type: Ammonia-Free, Demi-Permanent Color (alkaline cream toners + 2-5 minute fast toners; pre-blended 100% oxidative dyes; Certified Vegan)
developer_options: Matrix Cream Developer 20 Volume (6%) (recommended for gray blending on up to 75% gray hair)
developer_strengths_if_stated: Gray blending on up to 75% gray: 20 Volume (6%) Matrix Cream Developer, adding Neutral, Gold or Warm Neutral shades
Immediately following a Matrix Opti brand texture service: mixed only with 10 volume developer
processing_time_ranges: Alkaline Toners: 20 minutes room temperature ('Up to 20 minutes at Room temperature' in At a Glance)
Fast Toners: 2-5 minutes at the back bar
Clear: up to 20 minutes room temperature (*process 20 minutes for longest lasting results)
Refreshing over permanent color: process as needed, up to 20 minutes
Immediately after an Opti texture service: up to 10 minutes, checking frequently
gray_coverage_rules: 'SoColor Sync should not be used for gray coverage, but should be used for gray blending and to refresh coverage shades once they have grown out to the mid-lengths and ends.'
FAQ: 'SoColor Sync is ideal for gray blending and provides up to 75% gray coverage.'
Gray blending: formulate within one level of the natural pigmented hair; recommended shades alone or add any other SoColor Sync shade in a 3:1 ratio.
Gray Blending service result: 'Up to 75% gray blending'.
HD Series: will not cover gray; gray-blending table also flags Pearl 'Icy Cool - Does Not Cover' and Violet 'Soft Violet - Does Not Cover'.
lift_rules: Formulated with no lift; 'It is possible to see a 1/2 level of base shift with SoColor Sync depending on condition of the hair.'
Matrix does not recommend using heat.

tone_family_legend.meaning: Use with other shades to reduce tonal deposit by 1 level when mixed equally. Use alone to add shine without changing tone. Available in Alkaline and Acidic.
Palest level of each shade without any pre-blended base tone.
Pre-tone to neutralize warmth and soften tones before creating neutral, cool or fashion tones.

special_usage_notes: Apply to clean dry hair (Alkaline; 'clean 80% minimum dry hair' per usage section); Fast Toners to clean towel-dried hair at the back bar.
Long-lasting: 20 washes (Sheer Pastels 25) + fades on tone; shine/conditioning claim up to 20 shampoos.
Intermixing: Alkaline + Alkaline or Fast + Fast only; Alkaline Clear may dilute a Fast shade 1:1; HD Series only within itself; SoColor Sync Alkaline shades mix with 'Only other Alkaline Shades'.
Adding an equal amount of Clear to any SoColor Sync shade lessens color deposit by approximately one level.

Refreshing rules over SoColor permanent: choose pre-paired SoColor Sync shade 1-2 levels lighter than the permanent formula, same tonal family or slightly warmer; UL Series bases refresh with SoColor Sync Sheer Pastels.
Pre-Paired with SoColor: permanent and demi oxidative dyes matched for seamless scalp-to-ends color.
Wait 2 weeks after Opti.smooth heat straightening / sodium hydroxide / guanidine / calcium relaxer; may be applied immediately after a Matrix Opti texture service with 10 volume only.

Fast Toners: Anti-Red (levels 5 & below, neutralizes red), Anti-Brass (levels 6-8, neutralizes orange + brass), Anti-Yellow (levels 9 & above, neutralizes yellow); >5 minutes risks over-deposit and level shift.
Overly porous hair: lightly mist Instacure on mids + ends before blending through.
line_technical_records.source_document: 2023 Matrix Manual Haircolor (official PDF, 82 pages, cover: '2023 Manual Haircolor')
assumptions: Tonal Control Pre-Bonded (acidic gel toners) treated as a separate product line and excluded from SoColor Sync shade records, per the manual's separate branding; the shared 'Clear' is recorded once with its 'Available in Alkaline & Acidic' footnote.
data_gaps: The manual prints the reflect/tone legend keyed to digits (.0-.9), not to a letter-by-letter table; letter meanings beyond those explicitly decoded (UL, A+, SR, R, HD, EBC, SP, N/J primary-secondary) are not printed and were not inferred.

Fundamentals section (p.155) prints a variant digit legend: '.3 Gold/Warm' and '.5 Brown' versus '.3 Gold' and '.5 Mahogany' in the SoColor/SoColor Sync decoding sections (pp.57-58, 107-108). Decoding-section wording used in tone_family_legend.
Manual 'At a Glance' states 69 alkaline shades + 3 fast toners; palette-chart enumeration yields 70-71 codes (including HD-CR seen only on the HD tips page and Clear); discrepancy unresolved.
No per-shade names, swatch confirmations, or discontinued-status statements in the manual.
Official matrix.com SoColor Sync product page rendered no shade or technical data in static fetch (JavaScript-rendered).

issue_log: All line-level technical data transcribed from the official 2023 Matrix Manual Haircolor PDF (matrix.com, US/USMX media path).
Matrix Cream Developer lineup shown in manual: 10, 20, 30, 40 Volume (plus Lift+Tone Promoter 22 Volume and Coil Color 20+30 Volume developers, which belong to other lines).
No publication month printed in the PDF; cover states '2023 Manual Haircolor'. Several Blended palettes marked 'Launching June 2023'.
Discontinued status not addressed anywhere in the sources; left null (as of research date June 2026 the 2023 manual was still the current manual surfaced by matrix.com search).
